_note: SYNTHETIC SAMPLE DATA — fictional professors, not real SBU faculty. Shape mirrors RateMyProfessors GraphQL output. Real scraped data can replace this file using the same loader.

reviews.comment: Exams were brutal & the curve barely helped. Lectures move fast and he assumes you already know pointers. That said, office hours were a lifesaver — he's genuinely patient one-on-one. Do the homework early.
Tough grader. The programming assignments take forever and partial credit is rare. If you want an easy A this is not the section, but you will actually learn data structures inside out.
Fair but demanding. Midterms are conceptual, finals are coding-heavy. Keep up with the weekly problem sets and you'll be fine. Attendance isn't taken.
Best intro professor I've had. She explains OOP concepts slowly and with tons of examples. Perfect for someone who has never coded before. Highly recommend for your first CS class.
Super engaging lectures, lots of live coding. Quizzes are easy if you show up. She really wants beginners to succeed and curves generously.
Took her for 214 after 114. Still great, but the jump in workload is real. Exams are fair and she posts detailed solutions. Would take again.
Systems Fundamentals with Osei is dense. He basically reads from slides but the slides are good. Exams are cumulative and conceptual — memorizing won't save you, you have to understand the assembly.
Monotone lectures, hard to stay awake. Content is important but delivery is rough. The labs are where you actually learn. Bring questions to office hours because the lectures alone aren't enough.
Great gen-ed CS course. Participation counts via in-class clicker questions, so show up. Easy if you do the small weekly assignments. Good for non-majors.
Attendance basically matters because of clicker points. Lectures are light and fun. Don't expect deep programming, it's a broad overview.
